svg.title: samaydlette.com authorization boundary with data flows and FIPS modules
svg.desc: A FedRAMP authorization-boundary diagram for samaydlette.com. Shows the cloud service offering's internal components, services leveraged from AWS under FedRAMP authorization AGENCYAMAZONEW, external services without FedRAMP authorization that are inside the boundary per Rule of Thumb #2, out-of-boundary items per Rules of Thumb #3 and #4, the seven major data flows lettered A through G with ingress and egress points, FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptographic modules in scope, and a legend.
svg.text: Per FedRAMP “A FedRAMP Authorization Boundary” guidance · CSO targeting Rev 5 Moderate / 20x Class C
Per Key Concept #6: external services with a pre-existing FedRAMP authorization, inherited under the customer responsibility matrix.
EC2 / VPC networking / KMS / IAM control plane / S3 storage substrate / CloudFront edge / Lambda host / EventBridge / ACM PKI / CloudTrail backplane
Reached from CI via the AWS service APIs (HTTPS 443, TLS 1.2+, AWS SigV4). All deploys traverse this band.
FedRAMP authorization package: AGENCYAMAZONEW (AWS East/West Moderate)
Per Key Concept #1 + Rule of Thumb #1: components the CSP controls, plus the “federal information” surface (here scoped to the published artifacts at /.well-known/).
Public CDN; TLS 1.2+; OAC to S3
cdn_distribution · egress edge to public Internet
SSE-S3 at rest (AES-256, KMS-backed); public access blocked;
read access only via CloudFront OAC
rate(1 day) trigger for the Lambda
Lambda (non-privileged): read 3 S3 config APIs + 1 CF distribution; write 1 S3 key
External services without FedRAMP ATO — in boundary per Rule of Thumb #2

Each affects the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of system artifacts. Per Key Concept #5 + ROT #2, these must be inside the boundary even though they have no separate FedRAMP authorization.
Issues short-lived X.509 cert per CI run
Identity bound to GitHub Actions OIDC
Public transparency log of every signature
Branch protection on main + OPA gate
MFA on AWS root + GitHub account
FIPS 140-2/3 validated cryptographic modules in scope
Required by the FedRAMP Authorization Boundary Diagram convention. Lists the cryptographic modules that protect federal data and metadata in transit and at rest.
• AWS KMS — FIPS 140-3 Level 3 (HSM-backed CMKs); used for S3 SSE, EBS encryption, Secrets Manager
• ACM TLS termination on CloudFront — FIPS-validated cipher suites; TLS 1.2+ enforced
• S3 server-side encryption — AES-256 via KMS-backed module (data at rest)

text.tspan: External, no FedRAMP ATO (compensating controls documented):

svg.text: • Sigstore Fulcio + Rekor — ECDSA P-256 (FIPS-approved algorithm); the implementations are not
FIPS-validated modules. Trust anchored in the public Rekor transparency log.
• Lambda Node.js runtime — OpenSSL; no federal data processed in this PoC.
Algorithms in use across the system: AES-256-GCM (S3, EBS) · ECDSA P-256 (Sigstore signatures) · SHA-256 (artifact hashing)
TLS profile: TLS 1.2+ on all public endpoints (CloudFront, AWS APIs, GitHub, Sigstore, Duo)
Data at rest: all S3 objects encrypted via SSE-S3 with KMS-backed keys; CloudWatch Logs and CloudTrail use AWS-default at-rest encryption
Local laptop where code is authored.
Out per Rule of Thumb #4 (dev environment without federal info).
Out per Rule of Thumb #3 (corporate services not affecting CIA).
Anyone fetching the site or running
Consumers of public artifacts; not a system component.
Conceptually in scope at any future point a customer is onboarded.

g.text: Authorization boundary (red dashed border per FedRAMP convention)
Leveraged services with FedRAMP authorization (sp-inherited; AGENCYAMAZONEW)
External services without FedRAMP ATO — in boundary per Rule of Thumb #2
Out of boundary (Rule of Thumb #3 corporate services and #4 dev environments)
In transit: TLS · At rest: SSE-S3 + KMS
In transit: TLS · At rest: KMS
CSR, ECDSA P-256 signature, log entry
In transit: TLS · At rest: SSE-S3 + KMS
Trigger event; runtime KSI signal write
